statement: process_observability is the dominant live telemetry hot row, and most related OCC hotspots are downstream effects of mirroring too many events into that row.
rationale: Convex Insights show recordProcessObservability as the largest recurring OCC source, and code inspection shows most process, batch, job, scheduler, and error events are mirrored into one row per process.
keyAssumptions: The hot-row pattern, not just random load, explains the recurring OCC cluster.
validationCriteria: Reducing mirror frequency or moving recent events off the hot row materially reduces process_observability OCC in future load tests.
critiquePoints: Some contention may remain unavoidable because multiple writers still need the same process milestone fields.
questions: Should milestone fields and recent-event logs be separated into different storage paths?
